medical_history.chief_complaint: R breast ILC with positive axillary nodes
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Ms. ___ is a ___ female with a strong family history of breast cancer and bloody nipple discharge. She reports no previous abnormalities until last ___, when she noted an enlarged right axillary lymph node after a systemic symptoms and hives. Diagnostic bilateral mammogram from ___ shows no suspicious masses or abnormalities. Ultrasound at this time noted oval horizontal lymph nodes measuring 1 cm in size in both axilla. Core biopsy of the enlarged right axillary lymph node on ___ was positive for metastatic carcinoma, likely mammary origin, ER positive PR positive HER-2 negative.
medical_history.past_medical_history: ___ hypothyroidism, fibromyalgia, severe fatigue/chronic fatigue syndrome, sjorgens disease, hypertension. GYN History: Menarche at age ___, menstruation stopped with ablation in ___.
medical_history.family_history: Mom (age ___, 4 maternal aunts (all before age ___), maternal grandmother and maternal cousin with breast cancer. 1 maternal cousin with ovarian cancer and a maternal male cousin with an unknown cancer.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals - Temp: 99.5, BP: 124/71, HR: 85, RR: 18, O2 sat: 96% O2 on RA. General: Well appearing, no acute distress.
